codeReview.summary: The fix commit (aa774f8) properly stages and commits the 5 previously untracked memory-store artifacts that the original scrutiny review identified as the blocking issue. The commit is meaningful (5 new files, 3835 lines) and addresses the original failure completely. All 11 memory-store tests pass, typecheck passes, and lint passes. The worker correctly staged only the memory-related files without pulling in unrelated changes from other features.

issues.description: ScoutObservationRecord and ScoutObservationQuery type additions to contracts.ts remain unstaged (carried over from original review). The discovery-memory-store.ts is self-contained with its own duplicate definitions and does not import from contracts.ts, so this is not blocking. However, these unstaged contracts.ts changes will need to be committed eventually to avoid confusion about which file is the canonical source for these types.

summary: PASS — the fix adequately addresses the original blocking failure. The original issue was that all implementation files were uncommitted with the handoff pointing to an unrelated commit (9d5a036). The fix commit (aa774f8) correctly stages and commits the 5 previously untracked files: discovery-memory-store.ts source, 2 discovery-memory-store.test.ts test files (state + browser), company-planner.test.ts, and listing-fingerprint.test.ts. The commit is well-scoped, well-documented, and all verification steps pass (11/11 tests, typecheck, lint). The only remaining issue is the non-blocking contracts.ts duplication from the original review — ScoutObservationRecord/ScoutObservationQuery types are defined in both contracts.ts (unstaged) and discovery-memory-store.ts (committed). Since discovery-memory-store.ts is self-contained and does not import from contracts.ts, this does not affect functionality.
